title: elymus repens subsp. calcareus
intro: Elymus repens subsp. calcareus, also known as the creeping wild ryegrass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Elymus repens, characterized by its ability to form dense mats and its preference for calcium-rich soils. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. In terms of health benefits, Elymus repens subsp. calcareus has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune system function. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
